questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The length of each side of triangle A is $270$ millimeters. The length of each side of triangle B is double the length of each side of triangle A. What is the length, in millimeters, of each side of triangle B?
questions.questionText: The length of each side of triangle A is $270$ millimeters. The length of each side of triangle B is double the length of each side of triangle A. What is the length, in millimeters, of each side of triangle B?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Carrie has c dollars and Marjani has m dollars. Carrie has 4 times as many dollars as Marjani, and together they have a total of \$40.00. Which system of equations represents this situation?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Anton is selling toys from his toy collection. When he started selling the toys, there were 2,800 toys in the collection. At the end of each week for 3 weeks after Anton started selling the toys, the number of toys in the collection had decreased by $\frac{1}{2}$ of the toys in the collection at the end of the previous week. Which equation gives the number of toys, $c$, in the collection $w$ weeks after Anton started selling the toys, where $0 \leq w$?
questions.questionText: Anton is selling toys from his toy collection. When he started selling the toys, there were 2,800 toys in the collection. questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A bank account was opened with an initial deposit. Over the next several months, regular deposits were made into this account, and there were no withdrawals made during this time. The graph of the function $f$ shown, where $y=f(x)$, estimates the account balance, in dollars, in this bank account $x$ months since the initial deposit. To the nearest whole dollar, what is the amount of the initial deposit estimated by the graph?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A rectangle has a length that is 73 times its width. The function $y=(73w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y>0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $73w$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A rectangle has a length that is 73 times its width. The function $y=(73w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y>0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $73w$ in this context?

choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet
The area of the rectangle, in square feet
The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet
The width of the rectangle, in feet

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: An entomologist placed an initial population of 40 Tenebrio molitor, a type of beetle, into a habitat and monitored the population over time. When the number of Tenebrio molitor in the habitat reached 170% of the initial population, the entomologist moved 75% of the Tenebrio molitor to a second habitat. How many Tenebrio molitor did the entomologist move to the second habitat at this time?
questions.questionText: An entomologist placed an initial population of 40 Tenebrio molitor, a type of beetle, into a habitat and monitored the population over time. questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Circles A and B are graphed in the $xy$-plane. Circle A is represented by the equation $(x+8)^{2}+(y-8)^{2}=64$ and intersects the $x$-axis at the point $(r, s)$. Circle B has its center at $(r, s)$ and has a radius of the same length as circle A. Which equation represents circle B?

The dot plots represent the distributions of values in data sets A and B.
Which of the following statements must be true?
I. The median of data set A is equal to the median of data set B.
II. The standard deviation of data set A is equal to the standard deviation of data set B.

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: On January 1, 2000, the population of a town was 26,255 and on January 1, 2010, the population was 26,950. The equation $10x+26,255=26,950$ describes this situation. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $x$ in this context?
questions.questionText: On January 1, 2000, the population of a town was 26,255 and on January 1, 2010, the population was 26,950. The equation $10x+26,255=26,950$ describes this situation. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $x$ in this context?

choices.text: The total increase in population between 2000 and 2010
The average increase per year of the population between 2000 and 2010
The percentage by which the population of the town increased each year between 2000 and 2010
The projected population of the town 10 years after 2010

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Cube B has a volume of $125$ cubic inches. The length of each edge of cube A is $k$ times the length of each edge of cube B. If cube A has a volume of $3,375$ cubic inches, what is the value of $k$?
questions.questionText: Cube B has a volume of $125$ cubic inches. The length of each edge of cube A is $k$ times the length of each edge of cube B. If cube A has a volume of $3,375$ cubic inches, what is the value of $k$?

questions.questionText: A right square pyramid has a height of 6 units and a volume of 128 cubic units. What is the length, in units, of one side of the base of the pyramid?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 27% every 14.07 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?
questions.questionText: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 27% every 14.07 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
A department store sells pants in three lengths: short, regular, and long. The table summarizes the distribution of pant length, by color, for all the pants in the store.
A pair of pants is selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a pair of pants that is brown, given that the pair of pants is regular length? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent.)
questions.questionText:
A department store sells pants in three lengths: short, regular, and long. The table summarizes the distribution of pant length, by color, for all the pants in the store.
A pair of pants is selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a pair of pants that is brown, given that the pair of pants is regular length? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent.)

questions.prompt: An equation of the line in the $xy$-plane that contains the points $(0, -8)$ and $(-2, 0)$ is $Ax + By = 9$, where $A$ and $B$ are constants. What is the value of $B$?

questions.prompt:
$(x+k)+\frac{9}{2}(y+t)+19=0$
$(x+k)-\frac{9}{2}(y+t)-19=0$
In the given system of equations, $k$ and $t$ are constants. The solution to this system is $(x,y)$. What is the value of $18(y+t)$?
questions.questionText:

$(x+k)-\frac{9}{2}(y+t)-19=0$

questions.prompt:

questions.questionText:

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$g(x)=\frac{1}{11}(6(2)^{x}+7)$
The function g is defined by the given equation. For all values of x, the value of $g(x)$ is greater than k, where k is a constant. What is the greatest possible value of k?
questions.questionText:
$g(x)=\frac{1}{11}(6(2)^{x}+7)$
The function g is defined by the given equation. For all values of x, the value of $g(x)$ is greater than k, where k is a constant. What is the greatest possible value of k?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
At a nature preserve, a wildlife biologist counted ducks from an observation deck at the same time each day for 41 days. The table summarizes the resulting data set, data set A.
The data value 13 was recorded in error and is removed from data set A to create data set B, which consists of the remaining 40 data values. Which statement best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?
questions.questionText:
At a nature preserve, a wildlife biologist counted ducks from an observation deck at the same time each day for 41 days. The table summarizes the resulting data set, data set A.
The data value 13 was recorded in error and is removed from data set A to create data set B, which consists of the remaining 40 data values. Which statement best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?

choices.text: The median of data set B is greater than the median of data set A.
There is not enough information to compare the medians of the two data sets.
The median of data set B is equal to the median of data set A.
The median of data set B is less than the median of data set A.

category.skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A quadratic function gives the estimated length of daylight $d(t)$, in hours, in a certain city $t$ months after March 1, where $0 \leq t \leq 7$. Based on this function, what is the estimated length of daylight, in hours, on March 1?

questions.prompt: A bank account was opened with an initial deposit. Over the next several months, regular deposits were made into this account, and there were no withdrawals made during this time. The graph of the function $f$ shown, where $y=f(x)$, estimates the account balance, in dollars, in this bank account $x$ months since the initial deposit. To the nearest whole dollar, what is the amount of the initial deposit estimated by the graph?
questions.questionText: A bank account was opened with an initial deposit. Over the next several months, regular deposits were made into this account, and there were no withdrawals made during this time. The graph of the function $f$ shown, where $y=f(x)$, estimates the account balance, in dollars, in this bank account $x$ months since the initial deposit. To the nearest whole dollar, what is the amount of the initial deposit estimated by the graph?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$y=-12x+16$
$y=-20x+24$
What is the solution $(x,y)$ to the given system of equations?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A rectangle has a length that is 73 times its width. The function $y=(73w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y>0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $73w$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A rectangle has a length that is 73 times its width. The function $y=(73w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y>0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $73w$ in this context?

choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet
The area of the rectangle, in square feet
The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet
The width of the rectangle, in feet

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: An entomologist placed an initial population of 40 Tenebrio molitor, a type of beetle, into a habitat and monitored the population over time. When the number of Tenebrio molitor in the habitat reached 170% of the initial population, the entomologist moved 75% of the Tenebrio molitor to a second habitat. How many Tenebrio molitor did the entomologist move to the second habitat at this time?
questions.questionText: An entomologist placed an initial population of 40 Tenebrio molitor, a type of beetle, into a habitat and monitored the population over time. When the number of Tenebrio molitor in the habitat reached 170% of the initial population, the entomologist moved 75% of the Tenebrio molitor to a second habitat. How many Tenebrio molitor did the entomologist move to the second habitat at this time?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: For the polynomial function $f$, the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane passes through the points $(-3, 0)$, $(1, 0)$, and $(4, 0)$. Which of the following must be a factor of $f(x)$?
questions.questionText: For the polynomial function $f$, the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane passes through the points $(-3, 0)$, $(1, 0)$, and $(4, 0)$. Which of the following must be a factor of $f(x)$?

questions.prompt: If $\frac{x-17}{18}=\frac{x-17}{6}$, what is the value of $x+17$?
questions.questionText: If $\frac{x-17}{18}=\frac{x-17}{6}$, what is the value of $x+17$?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: For what value of $a$ is $\sqrt[a]{r^{42}}$ equivalent to $r^{\frac{6}{7}}$, where $r>1$?
questions.questionText: For what value of $a$ is $\sqrt[a]{r^{42}}$ equivalent to $r^{\frac{6}{7}}$, where $r>1$?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In the $xy$-plane, line $s$ passes through the point $(0,0)$ and is parallel to the line represented by the equation $y=36x+6$. If line $s$ also passes through the point $(9,d)$, what is the value of $d$?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Circles A and B are graphed in the $xy$-plane. Circle A is represented by the equation $(x+8)^{2}+(y-8)^{2}=64$ and intersects the $x$-axis at the point $(r, s)$. Circle B has its center at $(r, s)$ and has a radius of the same length as circle A. Which equation represents circle B?

questions.testName: August 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
The dot plots represent the distributions of values in data sets A and B.
Which of the following statements must be true?
I. The median of data set A is equal to the median of data set B.
II. The standard deviation of data set A is equal to the standard deviation of data set B.

For how many of the 9 data points is the actual $y$-value greater than the $y$-value predicted by the line of best fit?

$TR=520$
The side lengths of right triangle $RST$ are given. Triangle $RST$ is similar to triangle $UVW$, where $S$ corresponds to $V$ and $T$ corresponds to $W$. What is the value of $\tan W$?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$1.5c + 3d = 108$
The given equation describes the relationship between the number of cats, $c$, and the number of dogs, $d$, that can be cared for at a pet care business during a week. If the business cares for 36 dogs during a week, how many cats can it care for during this week?

questions.prompt: Stefan purchased 500 feet of fencing. He used 80% of this fencing to surround a vegetable garden. How many feet of fencing did Stefan use to surround the vegetable garden?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: On January 1, 2000, the population of a town was 26,255 and on January 1, 2010, the population was 26,950. The equation $10x+26,255=26,950$ describes this situation. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $x$ in this context?
questions.questionText: On January 1, 2000, the population of a town was 26,255 and on January 1, 2010, the population was 26,950. The equation $10x+26,255=26,950$ describes this situation. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $x$ in this context?

choices.text: The total increase in population between 2000 and 2010
The average increase per year of the population between 2000 and 2010
The percentage by which the population of the town increased each year between 2000 and 2010
The projected population of the town 10 years after 2010

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Cube B has a volume of $125$ cubic inches. The length of each edge of cube A is $k$ times the length of each edge of cube B. If cube A has a volume of $3,375$ cubic inches, what is the value of $k$?
questions.questionText: Cube B has a volume of $125$ cubic inches. The length of each edge of cube A is $k$ times the length of each edge of cube B. If cube A has a volume of $3,375$ cubic inches, what is the value of $k$?

questions.questionText: A right square pyramid has a height of 6 units and a volume of 128 cubic units. What is the length, in units, of one side of the base of the pyramid?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 27% every 14.07 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?
questions.questionText: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 27% every 14.07 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
A department store sells pants in three lengths: short, regular, and long. The table summarizes the distribution of pant length, by color, for all the pants in the store.
A pair of pants is selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a pair of pants that is brown, given that the pair of pants is regular length? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent.)
questions.questionText:
A department store sells pants in three lengths: short, regular, and long. The table summarizes the distribution of pant length, by color, for all the pants in the store.
A pair of pants is selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a pair of pants that is brown, given that the pair of pants is regular length? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent.)

questions.prompt:

$(x+k)-\frac{9}{2}(y+t)-19=0$
In the given system of equations, $k$ and $t$ are constants. The solution to this system is $(x,y)$. What is the value of $18(y+t)$?

questions.prompt:

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$g(x)=\frac{1}{11}(6(2)^{x}+7)$
The function g is defined by the given equation. For all values of x, the value of $g(x)$ is greater than k, where k is a constant. What is the greatest possible value of k?
questions.questionText:
$g(x)=\frac{1}{11}(6(2)^{x}+7)$

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
At a nature preserve, a wildlife biologist counted ducks from an observation deck at the same time each day for 41 days. The table summarizes the resulting data set, data set A.
The data value 13 was recorded in error and is removed from data set A to create data set B, which consists of the remaining 40 data values. Which statement best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?
questions.questionText:
At a nature preserve, a wildlife biologist counted ducks from an observation deck at the same time each day for 41 days. The table summarizes the resulting data set, data set A.
The data value 13 was recorded in error and is removed from data set A to create data set B, which consists of the remaining 40 data values. Which statement best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?

choices.text: The median of data set B is greater than the median of data set A.
There is not enough information to compare the medians of the two data sets.
The median of data set B is equal to the median of data set A.
The median of data set B is less than the median of data set A.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Each 3.0-ounce serving of cheddar cheese and each 1.2-ounce serving of tuna provides about 1 microgram of vitamin B12. If a total of 1.7 micrograms of vitamin B12 are consumed from eating $x$ ounces of cheese and $y$ ounces of tuna, which equation best represents this situation?

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 52° and AC = 40. In triangle PQR, the measure of angle P is 52° and PR = 120. Which additional piece of information is sufficient to prove that triangle ABC is similar to triangle PQR?
questions.questionText: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 52° and AC = 40. In triangle PQR, the measure of angle P is 52° and PR = 120. Which additional piece of information is sufficient to prove that triangle ABC is similar to triangle PQR?

choices.text: The measures of angle B and angle R are 40° and 88°, respectively.
The measures of angle B and angle Q are 52° and 40°, respectively.

questions.testName: September 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A quadratic function gives the estimated length of daylight $d(t)$, in hours, in a certain city $t$ months after March 1, where $0 \leq t \leq 7$. According to the function, the estimated length of daylight is 12.66 hours 6 months after March 1 and the maximum estimated length of daylight is 13.91 hours 3.5 months after March 1. Based on this function, what is the estimated length of daylight, in hours, on March 1?
questions.questionText: A quadratic function gives the estimated length of daylight $d(t)$, in hours, in a certain city $t$ months after March 1, where $0 \leq t \leq 7$. According to the function, the estimated length of daylight is 12.66 hours 6 months after March 1 and the maximum estimated length of daylight is 13.91 hours 3.5 months after March 1. Based on this function, what is the estimated length of daylight, in hours, on March 1?
